pages.raw_text:
Howard Leuton, the engineeE
who was so seriously injured in
the Great Northern collision near
Hillyard last Thursday night, con-
tinues to improve at Sacred Heart
hospital, where today reports Of
his condition indicate possibility
of recovery.


Coroner Witter, so far as known,
has made no move to investigate
and place the blame for the COLI
sion. Leutons evidence, it is
claimed, will place all blame for
the accident upon railroad officials.


The Great Northern operator at
Hillyard claims that he called the
attention of Leuton to the fact that
train No. 701 had not arrived at
that depot and cautioned him to
avail its arrival. Railroad men say
that it Is possible that Leuton will
be found responsible for the wreck.
They say that all regular passen-
ger or freight trains had right Of
way over Leuton, and that it was
his duty to ascertain where No. 701
was before leaving Hillyard.
